item.title: Statement from Dario Amodei on our discussions with the Department of War
item.description: A statement from our CEO on national security uses of AI

item.title: How to Securely Erase an old Hard Drive on macOS Tahoe
item.description: Apparently Apple thinks nobody with a modern Mac uses spinning rust (hard drives with platters) anymore. I plugged in a hard drive from an old iMac into my Mac Studio using my Sabrent USB to SATA Hard Drive enclosure, and opened up Disk Utility, clicked on the top-level disk in the sidebar, and clicked 'Erase'. Lo and behold, there's no 'Security Options' button on there, as there had been since—I believe—the very first version of Disk Utility in Mac OS X!

item.title: Nano Banana 2: Combining Pro capabilities with lightning-fast speed
item.description: Our latest image generation model offers advanced world knowledge, production-ready specs, subject consistency and more, all at Flash speed.
